items.headline: LeBron James gets emotional about playing with son Bronny
items.blurb: LeBron James gets emotional about playing with son Bronny — For James, the NBA isn't just about professional basketball. It's also about family.

items.headline: Austin Reaves endures historically poor playoff shooting game in Lakers' loss
items.blurb: Austin Reaves endures historically poor playoff shooting game in Lakers' loss — Without a co-star to rely on, LeBron James and the Los Angeles Lakers suffered a 108-90 loss to the Oklahoma City Thunder in the first game of their second-round series.

items.headline: Pistons big men dominate Cavaliers frontcourt to take Game 1
items.blurb: Pistons big men dominate Cavaliers frontcourt to take Game 1 — A hard-fought game came down to the Pistons' control of the paint in a 111-101 win.

items.headline: The 'NBA Defensive Players of the Year' quiz
items.blurb: The 'NBA Defensive Players of the Year' quiz — The NBA began awarding the Defensive Player of the Year following the 1982-83 season. Since then, the award has been given out 42 times. How many of the 42 can you name in five minutes?

items.headline: Lakers' Jarred Vanderbilt gets diagnosis for gruesome finger injury
items.blurb: Lakers' Jarred Vanderbilt gets diagnosis for gruesome finger injury — Lakers forward Jarred Vanderbilt suffered a full dislocation of his right pinkie finger during Game 1 of their series against the Thunder on Tuesday.

items.headline: Lakers’ Vanderbilt suffers ‘gruesome’ injury to pinkie while trying to block shot
items.blurb: Lakers’ Vanderbilt suffers ‘gruesome’ injury to pinkie while trying to block shot — Los Angeles Lakers forward Jarred Vanderbilt injured his right pinkie when he attempted to block a dunk by Oklahoma City Thunder centre Chet Holmgren during Game 1 of their Western Conference semifinal matchup on Tuesday night.

items.headline: Cade Cunningham gives first comments after Pistons' Game 1 win over Cavaliers
items.blurb: Cade Cunningham gives first comments after Pistons' Game 1 win over Cavaliers — The Detroit Pistons pulled off a dominant win over the Cleveland Cavaliers on Monday in Game 1 of the Eastern Conference Semifinals, with a 111-101 final score in downtown Detroit.

items.headline: Pistons' buzzer-beaters make big impact in Game 1 win
items.blurb: Pistons' buzzer-beaters make big impact in Game 1 win — The Detroit Pistons made the second-fewest three-pointers in the NBA last season. They got a big boost in their Game 1 victory thanks to two tough threes that just beat the buzzer.

items.headline: Cunningham scores 23, Harris has 20 to help Pistons beat Cavaliers in Game 1
items.blurb: Cunningham scores 23, Harris has 20 to help Pistons beat Cavaliers in Game 1 — Cade Cunningham scored 23 points, Tobias Harris had 20 and the Detroit Pistons beat the Cleveland Cavaliers 111-101 on Tuesday night in Game 1 of their second-round series.

items.headline: Winners and losers from Tuesday's NBA playoff action
items.blurb: Winners and losers from Tuesday's NBA playoff action — The Oklahoma City Thunder and the Detroit Pistons each collected Game 1 victories to start their second-round playoff series.

items.headline: Celtics' Jaylen Brown gets big punishment from NBA for controversial comments on referees
items.blurb: Celtics' Jaylen Brown gets big punishment from NBA for controversial comments on referees — Boston Celtics guard Jaylen Brown is paying the price for his comments about the officiating during the team's postseason loss to the Philadelphia 76ers.

items.blurb: How Timberwolves' toughness on the road makes them lethal — In the same way that Anthony Edwards shunned expectations for medical recovery, the Minnesota Timberwolves had no regard for homecourt advantage in Game 1.

items.blurb: Joe Mazzulla Expected To Remain With Celtics, Win NBA Coach of the Year — Joe Mazzulla is expected to return as Boston Celtics head coach next season and is anticipated to be named NBA Coach of the Year in the coming days, despite the franchise's stunning first-round playoff elimination at the hands of the seventh-seeded Philadelphia 76ers. items.headline: Mike Brown's Offensive Vision 'Starting To Click' With Another Dominant Game
items.blurb: Mike Brown's Offensive Vision 'Starting To Click' With Another Dominant Game — The New York Knicks appear to be hitting their stride at the right moment, with the offensive system coach Mike Brown envisioned when he replaced Tom Thibodeau last summer now operating at historically efficient levels through their first seven playoff games. items.headline: Raptors coach Darko Rajakovic already looking ahead to challenge of next season
items.blurb: Raptors coach Darko Rajakovic already looking ahead to challenge of next season — If 46 wins and a playoff spot is the benchmark, the room for error for the Raptors is shrinking. Coach Darko Rajakovic said he’s up for the challenge, writes Michael Grange.

items.headline: LeBron James explains Lakers' approach to Thunder series without Luka Doncic
items.blurb: LeBron James explains Lakers' approach to Thunder series without Luka Doncic — Talking to Steve Nash on their "Mind The Game" podcast, the four-time NBA champion opened up on his team's approach to the Western Conference semifinals.

items.headline: Rajakovic, Raptors not putting any cap on Murray-Boyles’ development
items.blurb: Rajakovic, Raptors not putting any cap on Murray-Boyles’ development — Raptors head coach Darko Rajakovic breaks down Collin Murray-Boyles’ rookie season, why there’s no limits to his development, and what prompted him and the organization to absolutely fall in love with him as a player and person.

items.headline: Rajakovic breaks down Raptors’ overall season, looks ahead to busy off-season
items.blurb: Rajakovic breaks down Raptors’ overall season, looks ahead to busy off-season — Watch the full year-end press conference from Raptors head coach Darko Rajakovic to assess the team’s growth from the regular season to the playoffs, and why he’s super-excited for a busy off-season.

items.headline: ‘It’s about making simple plays’: What Rajakovic learned in playoff setting
items.blurb: ‘It’s about making simple plays’: What Rajakovic learned in playoff setting — Raptors head coach Darko Rajakovic talks about what he and the team learned from their first taste of playoff basketball together, and why it’s all about executing the fundamentals of the game at a higher level.

items.headline: How Raptors’ Rajakovic thinks Barnes can take another step this summer
items.blurb: How Raptors’ Rajakovic thinks Barnes can take another step this summer — Raptors head coach Darko Rajakovic says Scottie Barnes is only scraping the surface of what he can become, says he’s at 58 per cent of his potential, and talks about the growth he can achieve this off-season, that will allow him to take another jump.

items.blurb: Bulls Pursuing Celtics Assistant GM Dave Lewin For Front Office Role — Although Dave Lewin did not land the Chicago Bulls' executive vice president of basketball operations position, the organization intends to pursue the Boston Celtics assistant general manager for a front office role. Lewin's interview left a strong impression. One source described the internal push to bring him aboard in clear

items.blurb: Raptors Pursued Jaren Jackson Jr. Trade At Deadline But Couldn't Move Jakob Poeltl's $104M Deal — The Toronto Raptors made a serious push to acquire Jaren Jackson Jr. from the Memphis Grizzlies before the February trade deadline, but were unable to complete a deal due to the burden of Jakob Poeltl's remaining contract, according to multiple league sources. items.headline: Tom Thibodeau Eyes Coaching Comeback, Would Consider Return To Bulls
items.blurb: Tom Thibodeau Eyes Coaching Comeback, Would Consider Return To Bulls — Tom Thibodeau is actively pursuing a return to coaching and has expressed strong interest in re-entering the NBA, with league sources telling The Athletic that the 68-year-old is open to a reunion with the Chicago Bulls if the organization shares that interest. items.blurb: Bryson Graham Given Full Autonomy By Bulls To Rebuild Front Office — The Chicago Bulls selected Bryson Graham as their new executive vice president of basketball operations over several high-profile candidates, with team sources telling ESPN the 39-year-old stood out for his range of experience and ability to unify a front office widely viewed as disjointed under the previous regime. items.blurb: Taylor Jenkins Among NBA's Highest-Paid Coaches At Over $10M Per Year With Bucks — Taylor Jenkins has signed a long-term contract worth well above $10 million annually to become the next head coach of the Milwaukee Bucks, per league sources, making him one of the highest-paid coaches in the NBA. items.headline: Wild LeBron James fact emerges ahead of Lakers-Thunder series
items.blurb: Wild LeBron James fact emerges ahead of Lakers-Thunder series — LeBron James and the Los Angeles Lakers kick off Game 1 of their playoff series against the Oklahoma City Thunder on Tuesday, and when James takes the court, he'll notch another wild bit of trivia.

items.headline: Gradey Dick Open To Trade From Raptors
items.blurb: Gradey Dick Open To Trade From Raptors — Toronto Raptors wing Gradey Dick's third NBA season didn't go the way he had hoped. Despite playing in a career-high 76 games, Dick turned in his worst NBA season. His role in the Raptors rotation was reduced as the season went along, as Dick finished with the worst stats of his three-year career. Toronto may look to trade Dick this offseason, as a way to f

items.blurb: Pelicans Identify Darvin Ham, Steve Hetzel, Rajon Rondo, Sean Sweeney As Coach Finalists — The New Orleans Pelicans have reportedly identified four candidates as finalists for their open head coach vacancy. The finals are Milwaukee Bucks assistant coaches Darvin Ham and Rajon Rondo, Brooklyn Nets assistant coach Steve Hetzel and San Antonio Spurs assistant coach Sean Sweeney. This list does not in

items.headline: Is there any world where you can back Lakers vs. juggernaut Thunder?
items.blurb: Is there any world where you can back Lakers vs. juggernaut Thunder? — VSiN By The Books’ Dave Ross and Jensen Lewis handicap the two NBA Game 1’s in Round 2, where they basically give zero chance for the Lakers vs. the defending champs, and why they like the over in the first tilt between Cavaliers and Pistons.

items.headline: LeBron James: Game-high 27 points in Game 1 loss
items.blurb: LeBron James: Game-high 27 points in Game 1 loss — James posted 27 points (12-17 FG, 3-6 3Pt, 0-1 FT), six assists, four rebounds and one steal in 36 minutes during Tuesday's 108-90 loss to the Thunder in Game 1 of the Western Conference Semifinals. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Chet Holmgren: Double-doubles in Game 1 win
items.blurb: Chet Holmgren: Double-doubles in Game 1 win — Holmgren contributed 24 points (9-17 FG, 2-2 3Pt, 4-4 FT), 12 rebounds, three blocks, one assist and one steal across 31 minutes in Tuesday's 108-90 win over the Lakers in Game 1 of the Western Conference Semifinals. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Anthony Edwards Returns From Knee Injury Early For Wolves' Game 1 Win Over Spurs
items.blurb: Anthony Edwards Returns From Knee Injury Early For Wolves' Game 1 Win Over Spurs — Anthony Edwards returned from a bone bruise ahead of schedule, coming off the bench to score 18 points in the Minnesota Timberwolves' 104-102 road victory over the San Antonio Spurs in Game 1 of the Western Conference semifinals Monday night. items.headline: James Harden: Full stat line in Game 1 loss
items.blurb: James Harden: Full stat line in Game 1 loss — Harden notched 22 points (6-15 FG, 1-7 3Pt, 9-9 FT), eight rebounds, seven assists, two blocks and one steal across 35 minutes in Tuesday's 111-101 loss to the Pistons in Game 1 of the Eastern Conference Semifinals. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Cade Cunningham: Paces Detroit in Game 1 win
items.blurb: Cade Cunningham: Paces Detroit in Game 1 win — Cunningham totaled 23 points (6-19 FG, 2-5 3Pt, 9-11 FT), seven assists, three rebounds and two steals across 42 minutes in Tuesday's 111-101 win over Cleveland in Game 1 of the Eastern Conference Semifinals. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Ayo Dosunmu: Iffy for Game 2
items.blurb: Ayo Dosunmu: Iffy for Game 2 — Dosunmu (calf) is questionable for Game 2 of the Western Conference Semifinals against the Spurs on Wednesday. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Jason Kidd Has Four Years Remaining On Contract With Mavs
items.blurb: Jason Kidd Has Four Years Remaining On Contract With Mavs — Dallas Mavericks head coach Jason Kidd has four years remaining on his contract, sources told Marc Stein of The Stein Line. Kidd's contract has been extended each of the past two seasons as the Knicks and Lakers expressed interest in hiring him. The Mavs hired Masai Ujiri as the team's new lead executive on Monday. items.headline: Patrick Dumont Wanted Lead Executive With Proven Track Record, Willingness To Be Communicative
items.blurb: Patrick Dumont Wanted Lead Executive With Proven Track Record, Willingness To Be Communicative — Dallas Mavericks owner Patrick Dumont was looking for a lead executive with a proven track record and a willingness to be communicative to the public, sources told Marc Stein of The Stein Line. items.headline: Sabrina Ionescu Out At Least 2 Weeks With Ankle Injury
items.blurb: Sabrina Ionescu Out At Least 2 Weeks With Ankle Injury — Sabrina Ionescu will miss at least two weeks with a left ankle and foot injury, the New York Liberty announced on Monday. Ionescu, who suffered the injury on Sunday, will miss the Liberty's WNBA season-opener on Friday. Ionescu averaged 18.2 points, 5.7 assists and 4.9 rebounds in 38 games last season.
